---
title: ""
pagetitle: "Shreyas Meher"
toc: false
---

::: {.home-hero}
::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-7}
# Shreyas Meher {.hero-name}

[Postdoctoral researcher at Erasmus University Rotterdam. I study how governments control information online, and how democratic institutions erode.]{.hero-lede}

On the [TWIN4DEM](https://twin4dem.eu/) project (Horizon Europe), I build machine learning models of democratic resilience and executive aggrandizement in parliamentary democracies. My broader work uses fine-tuned language models and causal research designs to study censorship, digital governance, and political conflict, including the NSF-funded [ConfliBERT](https://eventdata.utdallas.edu/) family of models.

My research has appeared in *Political Analysis*, *Research & Politics*, and *Behavioral Sciences*. I received my Ph.D. in Public Policy & Political Economy from the University of Texas at Dallas in 2025.

::: {.hero-links}
[Research](research.qmd) [Teaching](teaching.qmd) [CV](CV.pdf)
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-5}
![](headshot.jpg){#img-me fig-alt="Portrait of Shreyas Meher"}

::: {.hero-contact}
[meher@essb.eur.nl](mailto:meher@essb.eur.nl)<br>
Rotterdam, the Netherlands
:::
:::
:::
:::
